core_concept: Simple Bar Graphs and Organizing Data
facts: In a bar graph, the tallest bar always shows the group with the most items, and the shortest bar shows the group with the fewest.
When data is organized into a bar graph, you can find which group has more or fewer by looking at how high each bar is, without counting each item again.
To make a bar graph, first count how many items are in each group, then draw a bar that is that many units tall to record the count.
misconceptions: A child might think that a bar with a brighter color or a larger picture inside has more items, even if it is shorter than another bar; but the height of the bar alone tells which group has more.
